channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal has not been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's exports in December up 10.7%, beating estimates as higher US tariffs loom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — China’s exports in December grew at a faster pace than expected, as factories rushed to fill orders to beat higher tariffs that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has threatened to impose once he takes office.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the latest effort to bring an end to Turkey's 40-year Kurdish conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Talks between politicians from Turkey’s pro-Kurdish party and jailed Kurdish leaders have been gathering steam as they try to end 40 years of fighting between the state and the Kurdistan Workers’ Party, or PKK.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: India kicks off a massive Hindu festival touted as the world's largest religious gathering
item.description: Millions of Hindu devotees and mystics have gathered in the northern Indian city of Prayagraj for the Maha Kumbh festival, which is the largest religious congregation on earth.
item.dc:creator: Sheikh Saaliq, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden and Netanyahu discuss the latest in the effort to reach an Israel-Hamas war ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Joe Biden spoke Sunday about efforts to reach a ceasefire and hostage release deal in the Israel-Hamas war, a sign of the intensifying push to reach a deal before Donald Trump’s inauguration next week.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EU ministers will consider easing sanctions on Syria at a meeting later in January
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — European Union foreign ministers will meet in late January to discuss easing sanctions imposed on Syria, the bloc's foreign policy chief said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BARAA ANWER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada's Trudeau urges US consumers to consider the harm of Trump's tariff threats
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Canada's outgoing Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Sunday suggested that President-elect Donald Trump's remarks about Canada becoming America's “51st state” has distracted attention from the harm that steep tariffs would inflict on U.S. consumers.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No pants? No problem. London subway riders bare their legs to brighten a gray winter day
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Mind the pants. Hundreds of Londoners headed down to the Underground on Sunday afternoon, stripped down to their underwear and travelled around a bit, trying to look as though nothing unusual was going on. As if.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Croatia's President Milanović wins another term after defeating ruling party candidate in runoff
item.description: Croatia’s opposition-backed President Zoran Milanović, a critic of the European Union and NATO, overwhelmingly won reelection for another five-year term on Sunday, defeating a candidate from the ruling conservative party in a runoff vote.
item.dc:creator: Sasa Kavic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uyghurs detained in Thailand say they face deportation and persecution in China
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A group of Uyghur men who were detained in Thailand over a decade ago say that the Thai government is preparing to deport them to China, alarming activists and family members who say the men are at risk of abuse and torture if they are sent back.
item.dc:creator: DAKE KANG and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine captures first North Korean soldiers, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky announced that his military has captured two North Korean soldiers fighting on the Russian side in the Kursk region, likely the first such development since Pyongyang dispatched soldiers to help with the Kremlin’s war in Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin slammed for ‘waiting’ to declare genocide in Sudan
item.description: The incoming Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee has blasted the Biden Admnin for waiting until just 13 days before their term ends to declare atrocities in Sudan’s war as genocide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela latest: Maduro sworn in for third term as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel launches new strikes on Yemen’s Houthi rebels
item.description: Israel’s military says it carried out new airstrikes in Yemen against what it said were Houthi rebel targets. Its statement Friday said fighter jets struck “on the western coast and inland Yemen,” a day after the Houthis launched three drones at Israel. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau says he suggested trading 'Vermont or California' in meeting with Trump
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said he and President-elect Trump briefly discussed his comments about making the neighboring country the “51st state” during his Mar-a-Lago visit in Florida in November.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala freed from detention in Iran
item.description: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala was released after being detained in Iran for three weeks. Sala's plight became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation
